content_en:
Further, the two young cities,Al-Basrah and Al-Kufah, had ambitions of their own. They wished to expand their terrains to include neighbouring provinces by launching more battles and gaining more booty, hence improving their conditions. It was Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qais who suggested to the Caliph waging war against the Persians in the hearts of Khurasaan and cornering Yazdagird. When ~Umar granted his permission, Muslims fearlessly conquered one Persian city right after the other to the point that the Persian Khosrau had no choice but to cross the river “and seek refuge in Turkistaan. Though the King of Turkistaan mobilized a massive army to back the remnants of the vanquished Persian army, the Muslims displayed such valor that baffled the enemy and rendered them unable to achieve the goal they were gathered to see through. That chapter drew down the curtain on the Persian empire, one of the two world powers then. The entire Persian empire was conquered by people who would worshipped none but their Sole Creator, Allah, Exalted be He. It was therefore during the reign of “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab that the downfall of the Persian empire took place. It is noteworthy that Yazdagird finally gave in to the fact that the days of his past glory were irretrievably over. He spent the rest of his life expatriated till he was killed by one of his compatriots during the caliphate of ~ Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan. Abu Hurayrah narrates: "The Prophet said: "Khosrau will be ruined, and there will be no Khosrau after him, and Caesar will surely be ruined and there will be no Caesar after him, and you will spend their treasures in Allah's Cause." (Reported by AlBukhari and Moslem.)

The Conquest of Jerusalem:

While *Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraaah and Khalid Ibn AlWaleed were marching northwards in Syria, ~ Amr Ibn Al-~ Aas along with Sharhabeel [bn Hasannah were heading for Palestine with a view to liberating Jerusalem from the hands of the

Byzantines. When they reached Ramlah, they were encountered by a host of Byzantines, led by Utrabone (Tribunus), the shrewdest, most cunning and most ruthless commander who was second in rank after Heraclius. He had stationed massive armies at both Ramlah and I[lia’a (Jerusalem) so they could all be under his unified command.

He similarly stationed a garrison at each of Ghazza, AlLid, Nablus, Jafa and Sabastiah. Having set the scene as such, Tribunus eagerly awaited Muslims’ arrival so as to bring them to their knees and take vengeance on them for what they did to them in Syria.

Being the subtle army commander that he was, ~ Amr Ibn AlAas realised the jeopardy of the situation he was up against. He knew that victory would certainly be difficult if Muslims plunged into war with the situation being the way it was. He therefore felt the need to consult the Commander of the Faithful and brief him on the precarious conditions he was faced with. “Umar then dispatched Mu*aweyiah Ibn Abi Sufyaan to Qaysariah and wrote him saying: "I have appointed you to conquer Qaysariah, so march to it, and supplicate to Allah to grant you victory and always repeat the words: There is no power nor strength save in Allah, the Sublime, the Supreme. Allah is our God , in Whom we place our trust, on Whom we pin our hope; He is our Lord. Allah is the best to protect and the best to help."

Mu*aweyiah Ibn Abi Sufyaan headed for Qaysariah, besieged it and scuffled with its people several times, during one of which they engaged in fierce fighting till Allah granted His servants victory over their enemies. He killed nearly a hundred thousand of them and sent one-fifth of the booty to ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him. The fall of Qaysariah meant that Tribunus could no longer hope for

succour in times of distress.

Shortly afterward, ~ Amr received the news that Tribunus was marching with his troops towards Agnadeen. ~ Amr was every bit as shrewd as Tribunus. He had _ two of his commanders, along with their troops, head for Ilia'a and Ramlah to distract the enemies garrison there and keep them busy so they would not be able to leave their places. At that point, ~Amr wrote to the Commander of the Faithful asking for succor. He wrote “Umar informing him of the situation. When the Caliph read ~Amr's message, he smiled and said to those around him: “Tribunus of the Byzantines is going to be attacked by Tribunus of the Arabs (i.e. “Amr); so wait and see how things will turn out." Succour was dispatched right away. However, the city of Agnadeen was practically invisible. ~ Amr realised that he would have to resort to some artful contrivance on his part. Disguised as a messenger of Muslims' army commander, he went to meet Tribunus. He talked to him, heard what he had to say, took a good look at the place and found out what he needed to know. Tribunus thought to himself: " I swear that this is ~Amr or the man_ whose opinion ~ Amr follows. Nothing would be better than to kill them." Tribunus thereupon called one of his guards and whispered to him to ambush the messenger and kill him. Figuring out the trick, ~ Amr therefore told him: "O Prince, you listened to me and I listened to you. | am only one of ten men whom * Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab sent to accompany this commander in all he does. I would love to bring them all here to listen to you and see what I did." Tribunus returned: " All right, go bring them to me." He then called another of his guards and whispered to him to hurry to the first and bring him back. ~ Amr therefore made it back safely to his troops. When Tribunus learnt that he really was ~ Amr Ibn Al- *Aas, he said: "The man duped me! I swear that he is certainly the shrewdest of all Arabs!" When “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab learnt of this contrivance, he wondered: "What an excellent

man you are, “Amr!" Ferocious fighting flared in Agnadeen and both sides suffered heavy casualties. Victory at Agnadeen spurred Muslims to march on to Jerusalem, which they besieged for a very longtime, after having made sure no assault was to be expected from the rear. However, Jerusalem was a city to be reckoned with and the blockade lasted for a long period of time. Tribunus then wrote to ‘Amr saying: "You are my friend and my counterpart, you enjoy among your people the same rank that I do among mine. I swear you shall not conquer any further of Palestine after Agnadeen. So go back and do not grow arrogant lest you should be defeated the way those who came before you were." Amr called a man who spoke the language of the Byzantines, sent him to Tribunus and asked him to listen to what Tribunus told him then convey it to him. * Amr then wrote Tribunus a message wherein he said: "I received your message and you are my counterpart and enjoy an equal rank as mine among your people. You know that I am the conqueror of these lands. Read this messeng of mine out loud before your ministers and escort." So when Tribunus received the message, he gathered all his ministers and read it to them publicly. They asked Tribunus: “How do you know he is not the conqueror of these lands? He answered: " The name of the conqueror is all three letters." (The name '* Umar" in Arabic is made up of three letters.)’ Amr's messenger returned to him with Tribunus’ reply so “Amr wrote to “Umar [bn Al-Khattaab saying: " I am tackling an insurmountable thwarting war and facing a territory that seems to be preserved for you. So what shall be your command?"

According to Ibn Katheer: Jerusalem adamantly refused to give in to the blockade for a long time. Eventually, they agreed to conclude a peace settlements with Muslims provided that the Commander of the Faithful comes to the city in person. When *Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab learned of their request, he consulted Muslims as to whether it would be a good idea for

him to head for Jerusalem. ~Uthmaan Ibn ~ Affaan was of the view that the Caliph should not do so in order to demean them and force them to give in. Conversely, ~ Ali Ibn Abi Taalib advised him to head for Jerusalem as this would boost Muslims’ morale who had rigorously besieged the city. “Umar was inclined to follow ~ Ali's opinion. That was why he appointed him to be in charge of Madinah while he was away and set out for Jerusalem, accompanied by Al--Abbaas Ibn ~ Abdul Mutalib. * Umar chose to travel on horseback so as to cover the distance at a faster pace. When he arrived at Al-Gabiah, he gave a long eloquent sermon to Muslim troops. Having struck a peace settlement with people at Al-Gabiah, “Umar Ibn AlKhattaab headed for Jerusalem. He had earlier sent messages to army commanders asking them to meet him at Al-Gabiah ona specified day. On the appointed day , the army commanders did head for Al-Gabiah, one after the other to meet the Commander of the Faithful. Yazeed Ibn Abi Sufyaan was the first to arrive. He was followed by Abu *Ubaydah and then Khalid Ibn AlWaleed. It is narrated that when Abu *Ubaydah received ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab upon the latter's arrival, he dismounted. Thereupon, ~Umar dismounted, too. Abu ~Ubaydah was about to kiss *Umar's hand, so ~ Umar prepared to kiss Abu *Ubaydah 's foot. Abu * Ubaydah had to stop and so ‘Umar stopped.

Later, all commanders arrived to see “Umar Ibn AlKhattaab with the exception of Sharhabeel Ibn Hasannah and ~Amr Ibn Al-’ Aas who were on the alert at Agnadeen. While “Umar was at Al-Gabiah, an army of Byzantine soldiers, with their swords drawn in their hands, arrived. Armed and fully prepared, Muslims immediately set out to confront them. *Umar pointed:" These are people who came seeking peace." The Muslim army marched towards them, only to realise that they were soldiers from Jerusalem who came asking to be granted security and reconciliation when they learnt that ~ Umar

Ibn Al-Khattaab had arrived. The Commander of the Faithful, may Allah be pleased with them, granted them what they requested, signed a security and reconciliation settlement, imposed on them the Jiziah, and set a few conditions mentioned in books of history. Tribunus had no choice therefore but to flee to Egypt, where he stayed till it was conquered at the hands of ~ Amr Ibn Al-* Aas as will be explained shortly.
content_id:
Lebih lanjut, dua kota muda, Al-Bashrah dan Al-Kufah, memiliki ambisi mereka sendiri. Mereka ingin memperluas wilayah untuk mencakup provinsi-provinsi tetangga dengan melancarkan lebih banyak pertempuran dan mendapatkan lebih banyak ghanimah, sehingga memperbaiki kondisi mereka. Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qais-lah yang menyarankan kepada Khalifah untuk melancarkan perang melawan orang-orang Persia di jantung Khurasan dan mengepung Yazdagird. Ketika 'Umar (ra) memberikan izinnya, kaum Muslimin dengan berani menaklukkan satu demi satu kota Persia hingga Kisra Persia tidak punya pilihan selain menyeberangi sungai dan mencari perlindungan di Turkistan. Meskipun Raja Turkistan memobilisasi pasukan besar untuk mendukung sisa-sisa pasukan Persia yang dikalahkan, kaum Muslimin menampilkan keberanian yang membuat musuh kebingungan dan tidak mampu mencapai tujuan yang mereka kumpulkan untuk diwujudkan. Babak itu mengakhiri kekaisaran Persia, salah satu dari dua kekuatan dunia saat itu. Seluruh kekaisaran Persia ditaklukkan oleh orang-orang yang tidak menyembah selain Sang Pencipta Tunggal mereka, Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala. Oleh karena itu, selama pemerintahan 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) itulah kehancuran kekaisaran Persia terjadi. Perlu dicatat bahwa Yazdagird akhirnya menerima kenyataan bahwa hari-hari kejayaan masa lalunya sudah berlalu selamanya. Beliau menghabiskan sisa hidupnya dalam pengasingan hingga akhirnya dibunuh oleh salah seorang bangsanya sendiri pada masa kekhalifahan 'Utsman Ibn 'Affan (ra). Abu Hurairah (ra) meriwayatkan: "Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Kisra akan binasa, dan tidak akan ada Kisra setelahnya, dan Kaisar pun pasti akan binasa dan tidak akan ada Kaisar setelahnya, dan kalian akan membelanjakan harta kekayaan mereka di jalan Allah.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Penaklukan Yerusalem:

Sementara 'Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarrah (ra) dan Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra) bergerak ke utara di Syam, 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra) bersama Syarhabeel Ibn Hasanah menuju Palestina dengan tujuan membebaskan Yerusalem dari tangan Byzantium. Ketika mereka tiba di Ramlah, mereka dihadang oleh pasukan Byzantium yang dipimpin oleh Utrabone (Tribunus), komandan paling cerdas, paling licik, dan paling brutal yang berada di peringkat kedua setelah Heraklius. Beliau menempatkan pasukan besar di Ramlah dan Ilia' (Yerusalem) agar semuanya berada di bawah komandonya yang terpadu.

Beliau juga menempatkan garnisun di masing-masing Ghazza, Al-Lid, Nablus, Jafa, dan Sabastiyah. Setelah mengatur situasi demikian, Tribunus dengan penuh semangat menanti kedatangan kaum Muslimin untuk menaklukkan mereka dan membalas dendam atas apa yang telah dilakukan kaum Muslimin kepada mereka di Syam.

Sebagai komandan pasukan yang berwawasan tajam, 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra) menyadari bahaya dari situasi yang dihadapinya. Beliau tahu bahwa kemenangan pasti akan sulit jika kaum Muslimin langsung terjun ke dalam perang dengan kondisi yang ada. Oleh karena itu beliau merasa perlu berkonsultasi dengan Amirul Mukminin dan menginformasikan kepadanya tentang kondisi yang penuh bahaya yang sedang dihadapinya. 'Umar (ra) kemudian mengirimkan Mu'awiyah Ibn Abi Sufyan (ra) ke Qaisariyah dan menulis kepadanya: "Saya telah menugaskanmu untuk menaklukkan Qaisariyah, maka bergeraklah menuju ke sana, dan berdoalah kepada Allah agar menganugerahkan kemenangan kepadamu dan selalu ulang kata-kata: Tiada daya dan kekuatan kecuali dengan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi, Yang Maha Agung. Allah adalah Tuhan kami, tempat kami meletakkan kepercayaan kami, kepada-Nya kami menggantungkan harapan; Dialah Tuhan kami. Allah sebaik-baik pelindung dan sebaik-baik penolong."

Mu'awiyah Ibn Abi Sufyan (ra) menuju Qaisariyah, mengepungnya dan beberapa kali terlibat pertempuran dengan penduduknya, dalam salah satunya mereka terlibat dalam pertempuran sengit hingga Allah menganugerahkan kemenangan kepada hamba-hamba-Nya atas musuh-musuh mereka. Beliau membunuh hampir seratus ribu dari mereka dan mengirimkan seperlima ghanimah kepada 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra). Jatuhnya Qaisariyah berarti Tribunus tidak lagi bisa berharap mendapat bantuan dalam keadaan susah.

Tak lama kemudian, 'Amr (ra) menerima berita bahwa Tribunus sedang berbaris dengan pasukannya menuju Agnadein. 'Amr (ra) sama cerdasnya dengan Tribunus. Beliau menyuruh dua komandannya beserta pasukan mereka menuju Ilia' dan Ramlah untuk mengalihkan perhatian garnisun musuh di sana dan membuat mereka sibuk sehingga mereka tidak bisa meninggalkan tempat mereka. Pada saat itu, 'Amr (ra) menulis kepada Amirul Mukminin meminta bala bantuan. Beliau menulis kepada 'Umar (ra) menginformasikan tentang situasi tersebut. Ketika Khalifah membaca pesan 'Amr (ra), beliau tersenyum dan berkata kepada orang-orang di sekitarnya: "Tribunus Byzantium akan diserang oleh Tribunus Arab (yakni 'Amr (ra)); maka tunggulah dan lihatlah bagaimana hasilnya." Bala bantuan segera dikirimkan. Namun, kota Agnadein praktis tidak terlihat. 'Amr (ra) menyadari bahwa beliau harus mengandalkan kecerdikan tertentu. Menyamar sebagai utusan komandan pasukan Muslim, beliau pergi menemui Tribunus. Beliau berbicara dengannya, mendengarkan apa yang dikatakannya, memeriksa tempat dengan seksama, dan mengetahui apa yang perlu diketahuinya. Tribunus berpikir dalam hatinya: "Saya bersumpah bahwa ini pasti 'Amr atau orang yang pendapatnya diikuti 'Amr. Tidak ada yang lebih baik selain membunuh mereka." Tribunus pun memanggil salah seorang penjaganya dan berbisik kepadanya untuk menyergap sang utusan dan membunuhnya. Mengetahui tipu daya tersebut, 'Amr (ra) kemudian berkata kepadanya: "Wahai Pangeran, engkau telah mendengarku dan aku telah mendengarmu. Aku hanyalah satu dari sepuluh orang yang 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab kirimkan untuk menemani komandan ini dalam semua yang dilakukannya. Aku ingin membawa mereka semua ke sini untuk mendengarmu dan melihat apa yang aku lihat." Tribunus menjawab: "Baiklah, pergilah bawa mereka kepadaku." Beliau kemudian memanggil penjaga lainnya dan berbisik kepadanya untuk bergegas menemui yang pertama dan membawanya kembali. 'Amr (ra) pun berhasil kembali dengan selamat ke pasukannya. Ketika Tribunus mengetahui bahwa orang itu memang 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra), beliau berkata: "Orang itu telah menipu saya! Saya bersumpah bahwa dia pasti orang Arab paling cerdik!" Ketika 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) mengetahui tentang siasat ini, beliau bertanya: "Betapa hebatnya kamu, wahai 'Amr!" Pertempuran sengit meletus di Agnadein dan kedua belah pihak menderita korban yang besar. Kemenangan di Agnadein mendorong kaum Muslimin untuk bergerak menuju Yerusalem, yang mereka kepung dalam waktu yang sangat lama, setelah memastikan tidak ada serangan yang akan datang dari belakang. Namun, Yerusalem adalah kota yang harus diperhitungkan dan blokade berlangsung dalam waktu yang lama. Tribunus kemudian menulis kepada 'Amr (ra): "Kamu adalah temanku dan mitraku, kamu menikmati di antara rakyatmu kedudukan yang sama seperti yang aku nikmati di antara rakyatku. Saya bersumpah kamu tidak akan menaklukkan lebih jauh wilayah Palestina setelah Agnadein. Maka kembalilah dan jangan bersikap sombong agar kamu tidak dikalahkan seperti orang-orang yang datang sebelummu." 'Amr (ra) memanggil seseorang yang berbicara bahasa Byzantium, mengirimnya kepada Tribunus, dan memintanya mendengarkan apa yang Tribunus katakan kepadanya lalu menyampaikannya kepadanya. 'Amr (ra) kemudian menulis pesan kepada Tribunus di mana beliau berkata: "Saya menerima pesanmu dan kamu adalah mitraku dan memiliki kedudukan yang sama seperti milikku di antara rakyatmu. Kamu tahu bahwa sayalah yang akan menaklukkan tanah-tanah ini. Bacalah pesanku ini dengan keras di hadapan para menteri dan pengawalmu." Maka ketika Tribunus menerima pesan tersebut, beliau mengumpulkan semua menterinya dan membacakannya kepada mereka di hadapan umum. Mereka bertanya kepada Tribunus: "Dari mana kamu tahu bahwa dia bukan yang akan menaklukkan tanah-tanah ini?" Beliau menjawab: "Nama sang penakluk terdiri dari tiga huruf." (Nama "'Umar" dalam bahasa Arab terdiri dari tiga huruf.) Utusan 'Amr (ra) kembali kepadanya dengan balasan Tribunus, maka 'Amr (ra) menulis kepada 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra): "Saya sedang menghadapi perang yang sangat sulit dan berhadapan dengan wilayah yang tampaknya diperuntukkan bagi Anda. Maka apa perintah Anda?"

Menurut Ibnu Katsir: Yerusalem dengan keras kepala menolak untuk menyerah dalam waktu yang lama. Akhirnya, mereka setuju untuk menandatangani perjanjian damai dengan kaum Muslimin dengan syarat Amirul Mukminin datang sendiri ke kota itu. Ketika 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) mengetahui permintaan mereka, beliau berkonsultasi dengan kaum Muslimin tentang apakah ada baiknya jika beliau pergi ke Yerusalem. 'Utsman Ibn 'Affan (ra) berpendapat bahwa khalifah sebaiknya tidak pergi agar merendahkan mereka dan memaksa mereka menyerah. Sebaliknya, 'Ali Ibn Abi Thalib (ra) menyarankan agar beliau pergi ke Yerusalem karena ini akan menambah semangat kaum Muslimin yang telah mengepung kota itu dengan ketat. 'Umar (ra) cenderung mengikuti pendapat 'Ali (ra). Itulah mengapa beliau menunjuk 'Ali (ra) untuk bertanggung jawab atas Madinah sementara beliau pergi, dan berangkat menuju Yerusalem, ditemani oleh Al-'Abbas Ibn 'Abdul Muththalib (ra). 'Umar (ra) memilih untuk berkuda agar bisa menempuh jarak dengan kecepatan lebih tinggi. Ketika beliau tiba di Al-Jabiyah, beliau menyampaikan khutbah panjang yang fasih kepada pasukan Muslim. Setelah menandatangani perjanjian damai dengan penduduk Al-Jabiyah, 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) menuju Yerusalem. Sebelumnya beliau telah mengirim pesan kepada para komandan pasukan meminta mereka untuk bertemu dengannya di Al-Jabiyah pada hari yang ditentukan. Pada hari yang telah ditetapkan, para komandan pasukan menuju Al-Jabiyah, satu per satu, untuk menemui Amirul Mukminin. Yazid Ibn Abi Sufyan adalah yang pertama tiba. Beliau diikuti oleh Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) lalu Khalid Ibn Al-Walid (ra). Diriwayatkan bahwa ketika Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) menyambut 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) saat yang terakhir tiba, beliau turun dari tunggangannya. 'Umar (ra) pun turun. Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) hendak mencium tangan 'Umar (ra), maka 'Umar (ra) bersiap untuk mencium kaki Abu 'Ubaidah (ra). Abu 'Ubaidah (ra) harus berhenti, maka 'Umar (ra) pun berhenti.

Kemudian, semua komandan tiba untuk menemui 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) kecuali Syarhabeel Ibn Hasanah dan 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra) yang berjaga di Agnadein. Sementara 'Umar (ra) berada di Al-Jabiyah, sepasukan tentara Byzantium dengan pedang terhunus di tangan mereka tiba. Dengan bersenjata dan siap penuh, kaum Muslimin segera berangkat untuk menghadapi mereka. 'Umar (ra) menunjuk: "Ini adalah orang-orang yang datang mencari perdamaian." Pasukan Muslim pun bergerak maju ke arah mereka, hanya untuk menyadari bahwa mereka adalah tentara dari Yerusalem yang datang meminta keamanan dan rekonsiliasi ketika mereka mengetahui bahwa 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab (ra) telah tiba. Amirul Mukminin (ra) mengabulkan apa yang mereka minta, menandatangani perjanjian keamanan dan rekonsiliasi, memberlakukan Jizyah atas mereka, dan menetapkan beberapa syarat yang disebutkan dalam buku-buku sejarah. Tribunus tidak punya pilihan selain melarikan diri ke Mesir, di mana beliau tinggal hingga Mesir ditaklukkan di tangan 'Amr Ibn Al-'Ash (ra) sebagaimana akan dijelaskan sesaat lagi.
content_ar: Further, the two young cities,Al-Basrah and Al-Kufah, had ambitions of their own. They wished to expand their terrains to include neighbouring provinces by launching more battles and gaining more booty, ومن ثَم improving their conditions. It was Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qais who suggested to the Caliph waging war against the Persians in the القلوب of Khurasaan and cornering Yazdagird. عندما ~عمر granted his permission, المسلمون fearlessly conquered one Persian city الحق بعد the other to the point that the Persian Khosrau had no choice but to cross the river “and seek refuge in Turkistaan. Though the King of Turkistaan mobilized a massive army to back the remnants of the vanquished Persian army, the المسلمون displayed such valor that baffled the enemy and rendered them unable لتحقيق the goal they were gathered to see through. That الباب drew down the curtain on the Persian empire, one of the two الدنيا powers then. The entire Persian empire was conquered by people who would worshipped none but their Sole Creator, الله, Exalted be He. It was لذلك خلال the reign of “عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab that the downfall of the Persian empire took place. It is noteworthy that Yazdagird وأخيراً gave in to the fact that the days of his past glory were irretrievably over. He spent the rest of his الحياة expatriated till he was killed by one of his compatriots خلال the الخلافة of ~ Uthmaan Ibn * Affaan. أبو هريرة narrates: "النبي ﷺ said: "Khosrau will be ruined, and there will be no Khosrau بعد him, and Caesar will surely be ruined and there will be no Caesar بعد him, and you will spend their treasures in الله's Cause." (Reported by AlBukhari and Moslem.) The Conquest of Jerusalem: بينما *Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraaah and Khalid Ibn AlWaleed were marching northwards in Syria, ~ Amr Ibn Al-~ Aas along with Sharhabeel [bn Hasannah were heading for Palestine with a view to liberating Jerusalem from the hands of the Byzantines. عندما they reached Ramlah, they were encountered by a host of Byzantines, led by Utrabone (Tribunus), the shrewdest, most cunning and most ruthless commander who was ثانياً in rank بعد Heraclius. He had stationed massive armies at both Ramlah and I[lia’a (Jerusalem) لذا they could all be under his unified command. He similarly stationed a garrison at each of Ghazza, AlLid, Nablus, Jafa and Sabastiah. Having set the scene as such, Tribunus eagerly awaited المسلمون’ arrival لذا as to bring them to their knees and take vengeance on them for what they did to them in Syria. Being the subtle army commander that he was, ~ Amr Ibn AlAas realised the jeopardy of the situation he was up against. He knew that victory would certainly be difficult إذا المسلمون plunged into war with the situation being the الطريق it was. He لذلك felt the need to consult the Commander of the Faithful and brief him on the precarious conditions he was faced with. “عمر then dispatched Mu*aweyiah Ibn Abi Sufyaan to Qaysariah and wrote him sa
